response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with strong fundamentals in testing, distributed systems, and high-performance concurrent programming, but the fit for this specific C++ role is weak. Their hands-on C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis, with no recent C++ work. Recent roles are full-stack TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of message brokers, MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL secure engineering, industrial automation, or the listed industrial protocols, which are central to this position.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and a track record of high-throughput, low-latency systems
Demonstrated concurrency/async expertise (fibers, async I/O, microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Broad multi-language, full-stack experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and ~10 years stale; recent work is JS/TS and DLang
No exposure to message brokers, MQTT, REST IoT, or industrial automation domains
No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity practices required by the role

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial protocols, MQTT, message brokers
Strong concurrency and high-performance systems background
